definition: Conceit, especially the measuring movement of comparing oneself with others or with imagined standards.
notes: The project prefers conceit because it is the standard doctrinal rendering and keeps the term broader than ordinary pride. Measuring is useful in explanatory prose when the comparative function of māna needs to be made explicit rather than merely named. The OSF glossary strongly emphasizes comparison and hierarchy in explaining māna; the repository records that as source-backed explanatory framing.

context_rules.context: In most doctrinal and source-facing contexts
In explanatory prose emphasizing comparative self-measuring
